questions.q: What does the term 'Human Development Index (HDI)' signify?
questions.answer: A composite index that measures the average achievement of a country in three basic dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, knowledge, and a decent standard of living.

questions.q: Which of the following countries has the highest Human Development Index (HDI) value?
Explain the concept of 'Poverty Line'. How is it calculated?
questions.answer: The concept of 'Poverty Line' is a minimum level of income below which a person is considered to be poor. It is calculated by considering the cost of a basic basket of goods and services required for a minimum acceptable standard of living.

questions.q: What is the main criterion used to classify countries into three categories of HDI?
questions.answer: The Human Development Index (HDI) categorizes countries into three categories: Low Human Development, Medium Human Development, and High Human Development.

questions.q: What is the Human Poverty Index (HPI)?
questions.answer: The Human Poverty Index (HPI) is a measure of deprivation in three basic dimensions of human development: a long and healthy life, knowledge, and a decent standard of living.

questions.q: What is the main difference between HDI and HPI?
questions.answer: HDI measures the average achievement of a country in three basic dimensions of human development, while HPI measures deprivation in the same dimensions.

questions.q: How does the Human Development Index (HDI) account for income?
questions.answer: The Human Development Index (HDI) accounts for income through the Gross National Income (GNI) per capita.

questions.q: Explain the concept of 'Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI)'.
questions.answer: The Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI) is a measure of poverty that takes into account multiple deprivations in areas such as health, education, and living standards.

questions.q: Which of the following is a limitation of the Human Development Index (HDI)?
options: It does not account for income inequality
It only measures life expectancy at birth
It does not account for the distribution of income
questions.answer: It does not account for income inequality

questions.q: Explain the concept of 'Disability-Adjusted Life Years (DALYs)'.
questions.answer: Disability-Adjusted Life Years (DALYs) is a measure of the number of years lost due to poor health or disability.
